### Step 1: Check feasibility
If $k > \lfloor N/2 \rfloor$, return -1.

### Step 2: Handle Small N ($N=2, 3$)
If $N=2$: If $k=1$, return $\min(C[0], C[1])$. If $k=0$, return 0.
If $N=3$: If $k=1$, return $\min(C[0], C[1], C[2])$. If $k=0$, return 0.

### Step 3: General DP ($N \ge 4$)

**Case A: Index 0 excluded.** Path $1, \dots, N-1$. Length $L = N-1$. Target $k$.
$DP_A[L][k+1][2]$. Initialize $DP_A$ to $INF$.

Base case $i=1$:
$DP_A[1][0][0] = 0$.
$DP_A[1][1][1] = C[1]$.

Iterate $i=2$ to $N-1$. (Length $L=N-1$).
Iterate $j=0$ to $k$.

$DP_A[i][j][0] = \min(DP_A[i-1][j][0], DP_A[i-1][j][1])$.
If $j \ge 1$:
$DP_A[i][j][1] = DP_A[i-1][j-1][0] + C[i]$.

Result A = $\min(DP_A[N-1][k][0], DP_A[N-1][k][1])$.

**Case B: Index 0 included.** Path $2, \dots, N-2$. Length $L = N-3$. Target $k-1$.
If $k=0$, skip Case B (handled by Case A result already, and cost is 0).
If $k \ge 1$:
$DP_B[L][k][2]$. Initialize $DP_B$ to $INF$. (We need $k-1$ elements, so target $j$ goes up to $k-1$).

Base case $i=2$ (Path index 0):
$DP_B[0][0][0] = 0$.
$DP_B[0][1][1] = C[2]$.

Iterate $i'=1$ to $N-3$. (Corresponding cycle index $i = i'+2$).
Iterate $j=0$ to $k-1$.

$DP_B[i'][j][0] = \min(DP_B[i'-1][j][0], DP_B[i'-1][j][1])$.
If $j \ge 1$:
$DP_B[i'][j][1] = DP_B[i'-1][j-1][0] + C[i'+2]$.

Result B = $C[0] + \min(DP_B[N-3][k-1][0], DP_B[N-3][k-1][1])$.

Final minimum is $\min(\text{Result A}, \text{Result B})$. If both are $INF$, return -1.
